publications.title: Determining T-cell receptor binding orientation and Peptide-HLA interactions using cross-linking mass spectrometry.
publications.authors: Powell T, Karuppiah V, Shaikh SA, Pengelly R, Mai N, Barnbrook K, Sharma A, Harper S, Ebner M, Creese AJ.
publications.executive_summary: This study develops a cross-linking mass spectrometry method to determine TCR binding orientation to peptide-HLA complexes, validating the approach against crystal structures of two TCR–pHLA systems. The key finding is that XL-MS/MS can distinguish between canonical and reverse TCR docking orientations by mapping cross-linking sites to specific residues and identifying molecular interaction footprints. Since canonical binding geometry is essential for T cell activation while reverse-oriented TCRs are non-functional, rapid characterization of binding orientation is critical for understanding TCR specificity in immune response to pathogens and cancer surveillance. The method offers a faster and simpler alternative to structural biology techniques for assessing TCR–pHLA interactions, potentially accelerating research on T cell recognition and TCR engineering applications.
